name: science_question_scope
content: Good scientific questions are specific enough to test, but broad enough to matter.

name: science_hypothesis_design
content: A useful hypothesis states what is expected to change, under what conditions, and why.

name: science_correlation_causation
content: Correlation does not imply causation; causal claims require stronger evidence and alternative explanations must be considered.

name: science_control_variables
content: Controlling variables reduces ambiguity and makes experimental conclusions more interpretable.

name: science_evidence_strength
content: Evidence should be judged by source quality, sample size, bias, reproducibility, and consistency with other findings.

name: science_model_limits
content: Models are simplified representations of reality and should be evaluated by usefulness, assumptions, and limitations.

name: science_uncertainty_reporting
content: Clear scientific communication should distinguish observed facts, inferred mechanisms, and unresolved uncertainty.

name: science_explanation_layers
content: Complex ideas are easier to learn when explained in layers: intuition first, mechanism second, formal detail last.
